goal: Anchor a Franka articulation at /World/Franka so its base does not free-fall on Play, using anchor_robot with fixed_base=True. Verify via list_applied_schemas that ArticulationRootAPI is still on the original root prim and a PhysicsFixedJoint has been authored.
thoughts: Stationary-arm anchor pattern: use anchor_robot with fixed_base=True on the articulation root. Do NOT move ArticulationRootAPI off the root prim — PhysX tensor API pattern-matches on it. Wheeled robots (Carter, Jetbot) should NOT use fixed_base. After the call, verify the fixed joint exists.
code:
# 1. Confirm target
prim_exists(prim_path="/World/Franka")
list_applied_schemas(prim_path="/World/Franka")

# 2. Anchor (the one correct tool)
anchor_robot(prim_path="/World/Franka", fixed_base=True)

# 3. Verify: ArticulationRootAPI still on root, fixed joint authored somewhere under /World
list_applied_schemas(prim_path="/World/Franka")
count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/World", type_filter="PhysicsFixedJoint", recursive=True)
failure_modes: Using fixed_base=True on a wheeled robot (Carter/Jetbot) — kills the differential drive.
Moving ArticulationRootAPI to /World or another parent — PhysX tensor views fail.
Calling set_attribute on an invented 'physics:fixedBase' attribute instead of using the dedicated tool.
Forgetting to verify the fixed joint was authored — anchor_robot may no-op if the prim already had a fixed joint; user would never know.
